publisher.value: HL7 International / Biomedical Research and Regulation

description.value: The detailed description of a substance, typically at a level beyond what is used for prescribing.

short.value: The detailed description of a substance, typically at a level beyond what is used for prescribing

definition.value: The detailed description of a substance, typically at a level beyond what is used for prescribing.

map.value: Substance (and derived classes: Chemical, Polymer, Protein, Nucleic_Acid, Structurally_Diverse)

short.value: Identifier by which this substance is known

definition.value: Identifier by which this substance is known.

short.value: A business level edition or revision identifier

definition.value: A business level edition or revision identifier.

short.value: Status of substance within the catalogue e.g. active, retired

definition.value: Status of substance within the catalogue e.g. active, retired.

isModifierReason.value: This element is labelled as a modifier because it is a status element that can indicate that a record should not be treated as valid

description.value: The lifecycle status of an artifact.

short.value: A categorization, high level e.g. polymer or nucleic acid, or food, chemical, biological, or lower e.g. polymer linear or branch chain, or type of impurity

definition.value: A high level categorization, e.g. polymer or nucleic acid, or food, chemical, biological, or a lower level such as the general types of polymer (linear or branch chain) or type of impurity (process related or contaminant).

short.value: The applicable usage of the substance, as an example human or veterinary

definition.value: The applicable usage of the substance, as an example human or veterinary.

description.value: Applicable domain for this product (e.g. human, veterinary).

short.value: The quality standard, established benchmark, to which substance complies (e.g. USP/NF, BP)

definition.value: The quality standard, established benchmark, to which substance complies (e.g. USP/NF, Ph. Eur, JP, BP, Company Standard).

description.value: The quality standard, established benchmark, to which a substance complies

short.value: Textual description of the substance

definition.value: Textual description of the substance.

short.value: Textual comment about the substance's catalogue or registry record

definition.value: Textual comment about the substance's catalogue or registry record.

short.value: The entity that creates, makes, produces or fabricates the substance

definition.value: The entity that creates, makes, produces or fabricates the substance. This is a set of potential manufacturers but is not necessarily comprehensive.

short.value: An entity that is the source for the substance. It may be different from the manufacturer

definition.value: An entity that is the source for the substance. It may be different from the manufacturer. Supplier is synonymous to a distributor.

short.value: Moiety, for structural modifications

definition.value: Moiety, for structural modifications.

short.value: Role that the moiety is playing

definition.value: Role that the moiety is playing.

short.value: Identifier by which this moiety substance is known

definition.value: Identifier by which this moiety substance is known.

short.value: Textual name for this moiety substance

definition.value: Textual name for this moiety substance.

short.value: Stereochemistry type

definition.value: Stereochemistry type.

description.value: The optical rotation type of a substance.

short.value: Optical activity type

definition.value: Optical activity type.

description.value: The optical rotation type of a substance.

short.value: Molecular formula for this moiety (e.g. with the Hill system)

definition.value: Molecular formula for this moiety of this substance, typically using the Hill system.

short.value: Quantitative value for this moiety

definition.value: Quantitative value for this moiety.

short.value: The measurement type of the quantitative value

definition.value: The measurement type of the quantitative value. In capturing the actual relative amounts of substances or molecular fragments it may be necessary to indicate whether the amount refers to, for example, a mole ratio or weight ratio.

description.value: The relationship between two substance types.

short.value: General specifications for this substance

definition.value: General specifications for this substance.

short.value: The method used to find the characterization e.g. HPLC

definition.value: The method used to elucidate the characterization of the drug substance. Example: HPLC.

description.value: The method used to elucidate the characterization of the drug substance.

short.value: Describes the nature of the chemical entity and explains, for instance, whether this is a base or a salt form

definition.value: Describes the nature of the chemical entity and explains, for instance, whether this is a base or a salt form.

short.value: The description or justification in support of the interpretation of the data file

definition.value: The description or justification in support of the interpretation of the data file.

short.value: The data produced by the analytical instrument or a pictorial representation of that data. Examples: a JCAMP, JDX, or ADX file, or a chromatogram or spectrum analysis

definition.value: The data produced by the analytical instrument or a pictorial representation of that data. Examples: a JCAMP, JDX, or ADX file, or a chromatogram or spectrum analysis.

short.value: General specifications for this substance

definition.value: General specifications for this substance.

short.value: A code expressing the type of property

definition.value: A code expressing the type of property.

description.value: This value set includes all observable entity codes from SNOMED CT - provided as an exemplar value set.

short.value: A value for the property

definition.value: A value for the property.

short.value: The average mass of a molecule of a compound

definition.value: The average mass of a molecule of a compound compared to 1/12 the mass of carbon 12 and calculated as the sum of the atomic weights of the constituent atoms.

short.value: The method by which the weight was determined

definition.value: The method by which the molecular weight was determined.

description.value: The method by which the substance weight was measured.

short.value: Type of molecular weight e.g. exact, average, weight average

definition.value: Type of molecular weight such as exact, average (also known as. number average), weight average.

description.value: The type of substance weight measurement.

short.value: Used to capture quantitative values for a variety of elements

definition.value: Used to capture quantitative values for a variety of elements. If only limits are given, the arithmetic mean would be the average. If only a single definite value for a given element is given, it would be captured in this field.

short.value: Structural information

definition.value: Structural information.

short.value: Stereochemistry type

definition.value: Stereochemistry type.

description.value: The optical rotation type of a substance.

short.value: Optical activity type

definition.value: Optical activity type.

description.value: The optical rotation type of a substance.

short.value: An expression which states the number and type of atoms present in a molecule of a substance

definition.value: An expression which states the number and type of atoms present in a molecule of a substance.

short.value: Specified per moiety according to the Hill system

definition.value: Specified per moiety according to the Hill system, i.e. first C, then H, then alphabetical, each moiety separated by a dot.

short.value: The molecular weight or weight range

definition.value: The molecular weight or weight range (for proteins, polymers or nucleic acids).

short.value: The method used to find the structure e.g. X-ray, NMR

definition.value: The method used to elucidate the structure of the drug substance. Examples: X-ray, NMR, Peptide mapping, Ligand binding assay.

description.value: The method used to elucidate the structure of the drug substance.

short.value: Source of information for the structure

definition.value: The source of information about the structure.

short.value: A depiction of the structure of the substance

definition.value: A depiction of the structure of the substance.

short.value: The kind of structural representation (e.g. full, partial)

definition.value: The kind of structural representation (e.g. full, partial).

description.value: A format of a substance representation.

short.value: The structural representation as a text string in a standard format

definition.value: The structural representation as a text string in a standard format.

short.value: The format of the representation e.g. InChI, SMILES, MOLFILE (note: not the physical file format)

definition.value: The format of the representation e.g. InChI, SMILES, MOLFILE, CDX, SDF, PDB, mmCIF. The logical content type rather than the physical file format of a document.

description.value: A format of a substance representation.

short.value: An attachment with the structural representation e.g. a structure graphic or AnIML file

definition.value: An attached file with the structural representation e.g. a molecular structure graphic of the substance, a JCAMP or AnIML file.

short.value: Codes associated with the substance

definition.value: Codes associated with the substance.

short.value: The specific code

definition.value: The specific code.

short.value: Status of the code assignment, for example 'provisional', 'approved'

definition.value: Status of the code assignment, for example 'provisional', 'approved'.

description.value: The lifecycle status of an artifact.

short.value: The date at which the code status was changed

definition.value: The date at which the code status was changed as part of the terminology maintenance.

short.value: Any comment can be provided in this field

definition.value: Any comment can be provided in this field, if necessary.

short.value: Supporting literature

definition.value: Supporting literature.

short.value: Names applicable to this substance

definition.value: Names applicable to this substance.

short.value: The actual name

definition.value: The actual name.

short.value: Name type e.g. 'systematic', 'scientific, 'brand'

definition.value: Name type, for example 'systematic', 'scientific, 'brand'.

description.value: The type of a name given to a substance.

short.value: The status of the name e.g. 'current', 'proposed'

definition.value: The status of the name, for example 'current', 'proposed'.

description.value: The lifecycle status of an artifact.

short.value: If this is the preferred name for this substance

definition.value: If this is the preferred name for this substance.

meaningWhenMissing.value: If this value is not present, there is no statement being made about whether this is the preferred name or not. No inference can be made

short.value: Human language that the name is written in

definition.value: Human language that the name is written in.

description.value: IETF language tag for a human language

short.value: The use context of this name e.g. as an active ingredient or as a food colour additive

definition.value: The use context of this name for example if there is a different name a drug active ingredient as opposed to a food colour additive.

description.value: The use context of a substance name for example if there is a different name when used as a drug active ingredient as opposed to a food colour additive.

short.value: The jurisdiction where this name applies

definition.value: The jurisdiction where this name applies.

description.value: Jurisdiction codes

short.value: A synonym of this particular name, by which the substance is also known

definition.value: A synonym of this particular name, by which the substance is also known.

short.value: A translation for this name into another human language

definition.value: A translation for this name into another human language.

short.value: Details of the official nature of this name

definition.value: Details of the official nature of this name.

short.value: Which authority uses this official name

definition.value: Which authority uses this official name.

description.value: An authority that officiates substance names.

short.value: The status of the official name, for example 'draft', 'active'

definition.value: The status of the official name, for example 'draft', 'active', 'retired'.

description.value: The lifecycle status of an artifact.

short.value: Date of official name change

definition.value: Date of the official name change.

short.value: Supporting literature

definition.value: Supporting literature.

short.value: A link between this substance and another

definition.value: A link between this substance and another, with details of the relationship.

short.value: A pointer to another substance, as a resource or a representational code

definition.value: A pointer to another substance, as a resource or just a representational code.

short.value: For example "salt to parent", "active moiety"

definition.value: For example "salt to parent", "active moiety", "starting material", "polymorph", "impurity of".

description.value: The relationship between two substance types.

short.value: For example where an enzyme strongly bonds with a particular substance, this is a defining relationship for that enzyme, out of several possible relationships

definition.value: For example where an enzyme strongly bonds with a particular substance, this is a defining relationship for that enzyme, out of several possible substance relationships.

meaningWhenMissing.value: If this value is not present, there is no statement being made about whether this is a defining relationship or not. No inference can be made

short.value: A numeric factor for the relationship, e.g. that a substance salt has some percentage of active substance in relation to some other

definition.value: A numeric factor for the relationship, for instance to express that the salt of a substance has some percentage of the active substance in relation to some other.

short.value: An operator for the amount, for example "average", "approximately", "less than"

definition.value: An operator for the amount, for example "average", "approximately", "less than".

description.value: The relationship between two substance types.

short.value: The kind of measurement the amount represents (e.g. IC50, Kd, Ki, mole ratio)

definition.value: The kind of measurement that the amount represents, e.g. an inhibitory concentration (IC50), dissociation constant (Kd), inhibition constant (Ki), or a ratio expressed by mole or by weight.

description.value: The kind of numeric measurement (IC50, Kd, Ki, EC50, mole-ratio, weight-ratio, etc.).

short.value: For target relationships, the kind of interaction (e.g. binding, inhibition, agonism)

definition.value: For target relationships, the kind of interaction between the substance and its target.

description.value: The kind of interaction between a substance and its target.

short.value: For anti-infective target relationships, the specific organism targeted

definition.value: For anti-infective target relationships, the specific organism the substance is targeted against (e.g. E. coli, S. aureus). For broad-spectrum agents, organismType may be specified instead.

description.value: The specific organism that an anti-infective substance is targeted against.

short.value: For anti-infective target relationships, the high-level type of organism targeted

definition.value: For anti-infective target relationships, the high-level type of organism the substance is targeted against (e.g. bacterial, viral, fungal). May be used independently of organism, particularly for broad-spectrum agents.

description.value: The high-level type of organism that a substance is targeted against.

short.value: Supporting literature

definition.value: Supporting literature.

short.value: Material or taxonomic/anatomical source

definition.value: Material or taxonomic/anatomical source for the substance.

short.value: Classification of the origin of the raw material. e.g. cat hair is an Animal source type

definition.value: A classification that provides the origin of the raw material. Example: cat hair would be an Animal source type.

description.value: A classification that provides the origin of the substance raw material.

short.value: The genus of an organism e.g. the Latin epithet of the plant/animal scientific name

definition.value: The genus of an organism, typically referring to the Latin epithet of the genus element of the plant/animal scientific name.

description.value: The genus of an organism, typically referring to the Latin epithet of the genus element of the plant/animal scientific name.

short.value: The species of an organism e.g. the Latin epithet of the species of the plant/animal

definition.value: The species of an organism, typically referring to the Latin epithet of the species of the plant/animal.

description.value: A species of origin a substance raw material.

short.value: An anatomical origin of the source material within an organism

definition.value: An anatomical origin of the source material within an organism.

description.value: An anatomical origin of the source material within an organism.

short.value: The country or countries where the material is harvested

definition.value: The country or countries where the material is harvested.

description.value: Jurisdiction codes

short.value: Data items specific to nucleic acids

definition.value: Data items specific to nucleic acids.

short.value: The type of the sequence (e.g. DNA, RNA)

definition.value: The type of the sequence (e.g. DNA, RNA).

description.value: Whether the sequence is complete or partial.

short.value: The number of subunits — strands of nucleic acid that are tightly associated typically through Watson-Crick base pairing

definition.value: The number of subunits — strands of nucleic acid that are tightly associated typically through Watson-Crick base pairing.

short.value: The area of hybridisation, for double-stranded RNA or DNA. Expressed as the subunit number followed by the residue number, in increasing order

definition.value: The area of hybridisation, for double-stranded RNA or DNA. Expressed as the subunit number followed by the residue number, in increasing order.

short.value: Type or class of the oligonucleotide

definition.value: The class of oligonucleotide, where relevant (e.g. antisense, siRNA, aptamer).

description.value: The class of oligonucleotide (antisense, siRNA, aptamer, etc.).

short.value: A subunit constituting the nucleic acid

definition.value: A subunit constituting the nucleic acid. Subunits are listed in decreasing length order; ties are ordered by molecular weight; identical sequences are repeated.

short.value: Index of the subunit in decreasing length order

definition.value: Index of the subunit. Subunits are numbered in decreasing length order, with ties broken by molecular weight.

short.value: The nucleotide sequence in 5’→3’ notation using standard single-letter codes

definition.value: The nucleotide sequence in 5’→3’ notation using standard single-letter codes. Sugar and linkage type may be expressed alongside, via .sugar and .linkage.

short.value: The length of the sequence

definition.value: The length of the sequence.

short.value: The sequence as an attached file

definition.value: The sequence provided as an attached file (e.g. when too long for inline capture).

short.value: The nucleotide present at the 5’ terminus. Since the sequence is represented 5’→3’, this is the first nucleotide in the sequence

definition.value: The nucleotide present at the 5’ terminus. Since the sequence is represented 5’→3’, this is the first nucleotide in the sequence.

description.value: The nucleotide present at the 5′ terminus.

short.value: The nucleotide present at the 3’ terminus. Since the sequence is represented 5’→3’, this is the last nucleotide in the sequence

definition.value: The nucleotide present at the 3’ terminus. Since the sequence is represented 5’→3’, this is the last nucleotide in the sequence.

description.value: The nucleotide present at the 3′ terminus.

short.value: The linkages between sugar residues in the nucleotide sequence

definition.value: The linkages between sugar residues in the nucleotide sequence.

short.value: The chemistry of the linkage

definition.value: The chemistry of the linkage. For naturally occurring nucleic acids the linkage is usually a phosphate group; synthetic oligonucleotides may use phosphorothioate or other modified linkages. Linkage connectivity defaults to 3’-5’; 3’-3’ or 5’-5’ should be stated explicitly.

short.value: The linkage substance, as a code or a reference to its SubstanceDefinition

definition.value: The substance representing the linkage, expressed as a code or a reference to its SubstanceDefinition. Each linkage is registered as a fragment. Reference.identifier may be used to carry an external ID (e.g. ISO 11238 Substance ID) when no resolvable URL is available.

short.value: The residue positions at which this linkage applies, in 5’→3’ direction

definition.value: The residue positions at which this linkage applies, listed in 5’→3’ direction consistent with the base sequence.

short.value: A sugar component of the nucleotide sequence

definition.value: A sugar component of the nucleotide sequence, identifying the sugar substance and the residue positions to which it applies.

short.value: The sugar substance, as a code or a reference to its SubstanceDefinition

definition.value: The substance representing the sugar (or sugar-like component) of the nucleotide, expressed as a code or a reference to its SubstanceDefinition. Reference.identifier may be used to carry an external ID (e.g. ISO 11238 Substance ID) when no resolvable URL is available.

short.value: The residue positions at which this sugar applies, in 5’→3’ direction

definition.value: The residue positions at which this sugar applies, listed in 5’→3’ direction consistent with the base sequence.

short.value: The gene that codes for this substance

definition.value: The gene that codes for this substance (typically applies to proteins and nucleic acids). Captures the gene's identity and the source organism or lineage of the gene sequence (e.g. humanised mouse, chimeric mouse-human).

short.value: The species or lineage from which the gene sequence originated

definition.value: The species or lineage from which the gene sequence originated (e.g. human, humanised mouse, chimeric mouse-human, bacterial). For recombinant biologics, this is distinct from the production source organism.

description.value: The species or lineage from which a gene sequence originates.

short.value: The gene itself, as a code or a reference to a SubstanceDefinition

definition.value: The gene itself, expressed as a code or as a reference to the SubstanceDefinition describing the gene as a nucleic acid. Reference.identifier may be used to carry an external ID (e.g. NCBI Gene, HGNC symbol) when no resolvable URL is available.

short.value: Supporting literature for the gene information

definition.value: Supporting literature for the gene information.

short.value: Gene elements that comprise a nucleic acid construct (used for gene therapy)

definition.value: Information about the functional elements (promoter, enhancer, coding sequence, terminator, etc.) that make up a nucleic acid construct used in gene therapy. Each element captures its role and identity.

short.value: The functional role of the element (e.g. promoter, enhancer, coding sequence)

definition.value: The functional role of the element within the construct, for example promoter, enhancer, silencer, terminator, or coding sequence.

description.value: The functional role of a DNA element within a gene therapy construct.

short.value: The element itself, as a code or a reference to a SubstanceDefinition

definition.value: The element itself, expressed as a code or as a reference to a SubstanceDefinition describing the element as a nucleic acid substance. Reference.identifier may be used to carry an external ID (e.g. addgene plasmid ID, NCBI accession) when no resolvable URL is available.

short.value: Supporting literature for the gene element information

definition.value: Supporting literature for the gene element information.

short.value: Data items specific to polymers

definition.value: Data items specific to polymers.

short.value: Overall type of the polymer

definition.value: Overall type of the polymer.

description.value: Overall type of the polymer.

short.value: Polymer geometry, e.g. linear, branched, cross-linked, network or dendritic

definition.value: Polymer geometry, e.g. linear, branched, cross-linked, network or dendritic.

description.value: Polymer geometry e.g. linear, branched.

short.value: Describes the copolymer sequence type (polymer connectivity)

definition.value: Describes the copolymer sequence type (polymer connectivity).

description.value: Copolymer sequence/connectivity type.

short.value: A modification applied to the polymer

definition.value: A modification applied to the polymer, e.g. a chemical reaction that alters the structure post-polymerisation.

short.value: A set of monomers used to build the polymer

definition.value: A set of monomers used to build the polymer, with their proportions and relationships.

short.value: Captures the type of ratio to the entire polymer, e.g. Monomer/Polymer ratio, SRU/Polymer Ratio

definition.value: Captures the type of ratio to the entire polymer, e.g. Monomer/Polymer ratio, SRU/Polymer Ratio.

description.value: Type of ratio of monomers to the entire polymer.

short.value: The starting materials - monomer(s) used in the synthesis of the polymer

definition.value: The starting materials - monomer(s) used in the synthesis of the polymer.

short.value: The substance acting as starting material, as a code or a reference to a SubstanceDefinition

definition.value: The substance acting as starting material. This may be specified as a code or as a reference to another SubstanceDefinition.

description.value: Codes for substances acting as polymer starting materials.

short.value: Substance high level category, e.g. chemical substance

definition.value: Substance high level category, e.g. chemical substance.

description.value: High-level category of polymer starting material.

short.value: Whether this starting material is part of what defines the polymer's unique identity

definition.value: Whether this starting material is part of what defines the polymer's unique identity.

short.value: A percentage

definition.value: A percentage.

short.value: Specifies and quantifies the repeated units and their configuration

definition.value: Specifies and quantifies the repeated units and their configuration.

short.value: A representation of an (average) molecular formula from a polymer

definition.value: A representation of an (average) molecular formula from a polymer.

short.value: How the quantitative amount of Structural Repeat Units is captured (e.g. Exact, Numeric, Average)

definition.value: How the quantitative amount of Structural Repeat Units is captured (e.g. Exact, Numeric, Average).

description.value: How the quantitative amount of Structural Repeat Units is captured.

short.value: An SRU - Structural Repeat Unit

definition.value: An SRU - Structural Repeat Unit.

short.value: Identification of the structural repeat unit (SRU)

definition.value: Identification of the structural repeat unit (SRU).

short.value: The orientation of the polymerisation, e.g. head-tail, head-head, random

definition.value: The orientation of the polymerisation, e.g. head-tail, head-head, random.

description.value: The orientation of the polymerisation.

short.value: Number of repeats of this unit

definition.value: Number of repeats of this unit.

short.value: Applies to homopolymer and block co-polymers where the degree of polymerisation within a block can be described

definition.value: Applies to homopolymer and block co-polymers where the degree of polymerisation within a block can be described.

short.value: The type of degree of polymerisation, e.g. SRU/Polymer ratio

definition.value: The type of degree of polymerisation, e.g. SRU/Polymer ratio.

description.value: The type of degree of polymerisation.

short.value: An average amount of polymerisation

definition.value: An average amount of polymerisation.

short.value: A low expected limit of the amount

definition.value: A low expected limit of the amount.

short.value: A high expected limit of the amount

definition.value: A high expected limit of the amount.

short.value: A graphical structure for this SRU

definition.value: A graphical structure for this SRU.

short.value: Data items specific to proteins

definition.value: Data items specific to proteins.

short.value: Whether the sequence is complete or partial

definition.value: Whether the sequence provided is complete or partial.

description.value: Whether the protein sequence is complete or partial.

short.value: Number of subunits — linear sequences of amino acids linked through peptide bonds

definition.value: Number of subunits — linear sequences of amino acids linked through peptide bonds.

short.value: Disulphide bond(s) between cysteine residues on the same subunit or across two different subunits. Positions are listed in increasing order of subunit number and intra-subunit position, followed by the amino-acid abbreviations. The positions should contain Cysteine residues

definition.value: Disulphide bond(s) between cysteine residues on the same subunit or across two different subunits. Positions are listed in increasing order of subunit number and intra-subunit position, followed by the amino-acid abbreviations. The positions should contain Cysteine residues.

short.value: Each subunit constituting the protein. A subunit is a linear sequence of amino acids linked through peptide bonds. Used when the finished protein is a complex of multiple sequences (not for domains within a single sequence). Subunits are listed in order of decreasing length; ties resolved by decreasing molecular weight; identical sequences are repeated

definition.value: Each subunit constituting the protein. A subunit is a linear sequence of amino acids linked through peptide bonds. Used when the finished protein is a complex of multiple sequences (not for domains within a single sequence). Subunits are listed in order of decreasing length; ties resolved by decreasing molecular weight; identical sequences are repeated.

short.value: Index of primary sequences of amino acids linked through peptide bonds in order of decreasing length. Sequences of the same length will be ordered by molecular weight. Subunits that have identical sequences will be repeated and have sequential subscripts

definition.value: Index of primary sequences of amino acids linked through peptide bonds in order of decreasing length. Sequences of the same length will be ordered by molecular weight. Subunits that have identical sequences will be repeated and have sequential subscripts.

short.value: The amino-acid sequence, listed N- to C-terminal using standard single-letter amino-acid codes. Uppercase denotes L-amino acids, lowercase denotes D-amino acids. Transcribed proteins are described using the translated sequence; for synthetic peptides containing amino acids without a single-letter code, X is used. Modified amino acids are identified by their position in the sequence

definition.value: The amino-acid sequence, listed N- to C-terminal using standard single-letter amino-acid codes. Uppercase denotes L-amino acids, lowercase denotes D-amino acids. Transcribed proteins are described using the translated sequence; for synthetic peptides containing amino acids without a single-letter code, X is used. Modified amino acids are identified by their position in the sequence.

short.value: Length of linear sequences of amino acids contained in the subunit

definition.value: Length of linear sequences of amino acids contained in the subunit.

short.value: The amino-acid sequence as an attached file

definition.value: The amino-acid sequence provided as an attached file (e.g. when too long for inline capture, or only available in non-text form such as an image).

short.value: The modification fragment at the N-terminal of the protein

definition.value: The molecular fragment modification at the N-terminal of the protein, expressed as a code or a reference to its SubstanceDefinition. Reference.identifier may be used to carry an external ID (e.g. ISO 11238 Substance ID) when no resolvable URL is available.

short.value: The modification fragment at the C-terminal of the protein

definition.value: The molecular fragment modification at the C-terminal of the protein, expressed as a code or a reference to its SubstanceDefinition. Reference.identifier may be used to carry an external ID (e.g. ISO 11238 Substance ID) when no resolvable URL is available.

short.value: The gene that codes for this substance

definition.value: The gene that codes for this protein substance (typically the source gene). Captures the gene's identity and the source organism or lineage of the gene sequence (e.g. humanised mouse, chimeric mouse-human). Same structure as nucleicAcid.gene.
